title: Aluminum Drops as Trump Moves to Narrow Levies on Metal Products
summary: Aluminum fell as traders reacted to reports that President Donald Trump may narrow the scope of U.S. import tariffs on some aluminum and steel products. The move raised expectations that duties could be rolled back because of concerns about consumer impact, and aluminum was quoted lower on the London Metal Exchange during the session.

title: AI data centers' massive demand for aluminum is crushing the US aluminum industry
summary: A new aluminum smelter in Oklahoma with a capacity of about 600,000 tons per year is nearly doubling current US domestic production, yet only six smelting operations remain with just four actively producing. Even at full capacity, US smelters could only satisfy about a third of domestic demand, requiring five years and $25 billion investment to meet current needs.

title: US aluminium smelters vie with Big Tech for scarce power
summary: It takes 14,821 kilowatt-hours of electricity to make a ton of aluminium, and modern smelters are struggling with scarce power as the number of operational facilities has dwindled to just six. Annual production has plummeted to 700,000 tons with two smelters completely shut down and others operating below potential.

title: America's big new aluminum smelter is still waiting on a power deal
summary: America's new aluminum smelter expects to start construction in late 2026 and begin producing metal by the end of the decade, but finalizing the power deal remains a critical hurdle. The project represents a significant expansion of US aluminum capacity amid growing demand from data centers.

title: Major US aluminum producer boosts production to full capacity
summary: Century Aluminum has decided to boost production to full capacity amid President Donald Trump's policies supporting domestic manufacturing. This decision comes as the US aluminum industry faces challenges with only six remaining smelting operations and limited capacity to meet domestic demand.

summary: This paper reviews decarbonization technologies in the iron and steel sector, emphasizing new technologies that can significantly cut emissions. It discusses industry R&D efforts and sustainability-oriented transitions such as green hydrogen and other low-carbon process options.

title: Copper futures drop below $6.3/lb as Rio Tinto resumes Oyu Tolgoi exports
summary: Copper futures fell below $6.3 per pound on Friday after giving back recent gains. The move followed news that Rio Tinto resumed exports of copper concentrate from its giant Oyu Tolgoi mine, which eased some supply concerns.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: Bloomberg reports that PT Weda Bay Nickel, described as the world’s biggest nickel mine, has been told to sharply reduce output in Indonesia. The company was reportedly notified it would receive 12 million tons of ore production quota for the year, down from 42 million tons in 2025, as Indonesia seeks to support global nickel prices.

title: Nickel oversupply to persist on expansion, slower demand growth, industry experts say
summary: Reuters reports that global nickel oversupply is expected to continue for several years because production capacity is expanding while demand growth slows. The article says new projects in Indonesia are likely to increase output capacity further, with some analysts seeing the surplus lasting until 2027-2028.

title: Indonesia navigates nickel market with output cuts, policy shifts
summary: S&P Global reports that Indonesia plans to cut nickel production in 2026 to support prices and tighten supply. The piece says the policy is also aimed at curbing environmentally harmful operations and managing volatility in mining quotas.

title: Aosta, Cogne Acciai Speciali: bilancio in rosso e futuro incerto
